steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A semicolon joins two independent clauses. The second clause has the subject 'this function' and ends with the prepositional phrase 'from the biocompatible properties of these particles.' The blank is the main verb of that clause, so it must be a finite (tensed) verb form that agrees with a singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — stemming: a present participle. It cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice B — having stemmed: a perfect participle phrase. It cannot be the main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — stems: simple present tense, third-person singular — agrees with 'this function' and functions as a proper finite verb. ✓
  • Choice D — to stem: an infinitive. Like 'stemming,' it cannot anchor an independent clause. ✗
That is the correct answer.
